output.summary: Revision 1: Applied user-requested change to use WordPress media library alt text natively. Removed manual alt text override from wp_get_attachment_image() call in PHP. WordPress now handles alt text automatically from the media library, respecting whatever alt text the editor sets when uploading images.

changes: Line 146: Removed manual alt text lookup: `$img_alt = get_post_meta( $featuredImageId, '_wp_attachment_image_alt', true );`
Line 153: Removed 'alt' key override from wp_get_attachment_image() attributes array
wp_get_attachment_image() now uses default behavior which automatically includes media library alt text
filesModified.reasoning: The previous implementation manually overrode alt text with `'alt' => $img_alt ?: ''`, which was redundant since wp_get_attachment_image() already reads and outputs the media library alt text automatically. User explicitly requested: 'The WordPress media library alt text should be used as-is, since it may describe image content not conveyed by the heading.' By removing the manual override, WordPress handles alt text natively from the media library.

filesNotModified.reason: TSX already correctly uses `featuredImage.alt_text || ''` which reads the media library alt text from the WordPress REST API. The `|| ''` ensures no undefined values. No changes needed.

buildStatus.details: Build completed successfully with no TypeScript errors

syncStatus.details: TSX and PHP both use WordPress media library alt text. PHP uses wp_get_attachment_image() default behavior (reads from media library). TSX uses fetchMedia() which gets alt_text from WordPress REST API. Both respect the same source of truth: the media library alt text field.

fixesApplied.description: Removed alt text override to use WordPress media library alt text natively
fixesApplied.verification: wp_get_attachment_image() now called without 'alt' attribute override. WordPress automatically includes alt text from media library. Manual $img_alt variable removed entirely.

notes: User explicitly requested this change to use media library alt text as-is
Previous cycle incorrectly changed alt fallback from postTitle to empty string, but user wants media library alt text used directly
wp_get_attachment_image() handles alt text automatically - no manual override needed
TSX already correct - uses featuredImage.alt_text from WordPress REST API
Both PHP and TSX now respect the same source: WordPress media library alt text field
No functional changes to layout, styling, or other attributes

cssAuditSummary.question: Does FeaturedPost depend on CSS from other blocks?
cssAuditSummary.answer: No. All CSS classes come from Tailwind utilities or theme-level CSS files (colors.css, typography.css, spacing.css, button.css). No dependencies on Hero.css or ContentWithMedia. No FeaturedPost.css file needed.

imageMaskAuditSummary.question: Does FeaturedPost use inline style attributes or mask-image properties?
imageMaskAuditSummary.answer: No. FeaturedPost has zero inline style attributes and zero mask-image usage. The mask-image concern applies to PostHero block (PostHero.php line 46), not FeaturedPost.

verifications.details: $img_alt variable no longer exists. 'alt' key not present in wp_get_attachment_image() attributes array.
wp_get_attachment_image() called with only 'class' attribute, allowing WordPress to handle alt text from media library automatically
TSX already uses featuredImage.alt_text || '' which correctly reads media library alt text from REST API
bun run build completed successfully with only expected asset size warnings
Both PHP and TSX use WordPress media library as source of truth for alt text

userFeedbackAddressed: User concern 1: 'The WordPress media library alt text should be used as-is' - ADDRESSED by removing manual alt override
User concern 2: 'Does FeaturedPost depend on CSS from other blocks?' - VERIFIED: No dependencies, all CSS is Tailwind/theme-level
User concern 3: 'Image mask inline styles should be moved to CSS' - VERIFIED: Not applicable to FeaturedPost, applies to PostHero block only
